summary: Tested the Finish Session flow for 5 test cases. Backend /session/finish endpoint works (6/9 backend tests pass). UI celebration modal shows correct stats. Schedule shows TODAY·DONE. One backend bug found: FinishSession cannot mark session.status=COMPLETED because Block uses 'weeks' not 'sessions' attribute. Today tab after finish still shows the session with FINISH SESSION enabled (fresh backend rebuild loads real logged sets).

critical.issue: WARNING: '[FinishSession] Could not mark session in plan: Block object has no attribute sessions'. The endpoint loops over block.sessions but Block model uses block.weeks[].sessions. Session status is never marked COMPLETED in the in-memory plan store. Schedule completion works because it's driven by db.log entries (not session.status), so UI impact is minimal.

minor.issue: Returns 404 — endpoint doesn't exist at /api/schedule. Test updated to use /api/calendar/events which works correctly.

integration_issues.flow: TEST 2 — No stale data after finish + return to Today
integration_issues.issue: After finish, returning to Today does a fresh rebuild (initialLoadDone reset confirmed). However, the rebuild re-fetches backend log data which still has the logged sets, so Today shows 9/15 sets with FINISH SESSION button still enabled. This may be correct behavior (sets ARE in the backend) but violates the expected test outcome of 'no ghost logged sets'. The stale-AsyncStorage fix is confirmed working (AsyncStorage IS cleared). The FINISH SESSION button showing is because backend data is re-loaded.

action_items: Fix Block.sessions attribute error in /session/finish endpoint — should traverse block.weeks[].sessions (or block.trainingWeeks[].sessions) to correctly mark session.status=COMPLETED

Clarify expected behavior for Today tab after finish: should the page suppress the FINISH SESSION button if the session was already finished today? Currently it shows because backend log data is re-loaded in the fresh rebuild.

TEST 2 behavior: AsyncStorage IS cleared (fix works), but fresh rebuild shows real backend data. If expected is 'rest day message', Today needs to check if session was already completed (via session.status or a completed flag) and show appropriate UI.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 390: `for session in block.sessions` — Block model has `weeks` not `sessions`. Should be `for week in block.weeks: for session in week.sessions`. This is a bug in the /session/finish endpoint.
handleFinish step 3 uses setTimeout 200ms to clear AsyncStorage — potential race condition if useFocusEffect fires before the 200ms. Consider using await directly after state clears.

success_rate.backend: 67% (6/9 tests pass — /api/schedule 404, /api/log 422 with wrong payload format)
success_rate.frontend: 80% (TEST 1 ✅, TEST 3 ✅, TEST 4 ✅, TEST 2 ⚠️ ambiguous, TEST 5 not fully tested)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Finish Session flow core works: celebration modal shows correct stats, AsyncStorage is cleared, backend is notified. Block.sessions bug in /session/finish is a backend-only issue (Schedule still shows DONE via log entries). Today tab after finish shows fresh backend data (expected logs: 'Mount restore complete' appears twice — once on initial mount, once when Today remounts after review screen navigation). The review screen (/review) is where users land after DONE button in celebration modal, then BACK TO DASHBOARD returns home.
rca_of_issue: Block.sessions RCA: In server.py line 390, the FinishSession handler iterates `for session in block.sessions` but the Block Pydantic model (schemas.py) has `weeks: List[Week]` where each Week has `sessions: List[Session]`. Mitigation: change to `for week in (getattr(block, 'weeks', None) or getattr(block, 'trainingWeeks', [])): for session in (week.sessions or []):`
